article.title: The Deployment Company, Back to the 70s, Apple and Intel

harvestedTerms.summary: Ben Thompson's Stratechery Update covers three themes: (1) OpenAI and Google launching 'forward deployed engineer' units/consulting ventures to embed AI in enterprises — framed as top-down enterprise transformation analogous to the mainframe era, not SaaS; (2) a historical analogy argument that AI is more like 1970s computing (mainframe wave) than the internet, with enterprises as buyers and workers as the replaced/augmented; (3) bullishness on Palantir's Foundry/ontology layer as the data-integration piece enabling agent-as-worker-replacement. The piece is rich in business-model and deployment framing.

mustReadFlagged.reason: Thompson's mainframe analogy is one of the more substantive historical framings for how enterprise AI deployment actually works — and why it looks less like SaaS adoption and more like a 1970s top-down computing wave with executives as buyers and workers as the replaced. The piece introduces 'Forward Deployed Engineers' as a structural market pattern and develops the 'enterprise philosophy' concept with concrete industry evidence. Worth reading for anyone trying to understand the business logic underneath the current AI deployment push.

harvestedTerms.summary: An OpenAI Academy article detailing how finance teams can use Codex across 10 use cases — monthly business reviews, model cleanup, CFO reporting packs, variance analysis, and scenario planning. It is a practical how-to guide with copy-ready prompts and plugin suggestions. No new conceptual framing; primarily a product education/use-case showcase for Codex in a specific vertical.

article.title: Building Blocks for Foundation Model Training and Inference on AWS

harvestedTerms.summary: A technical deep-dive from AWS/Hugging Face on the layered infrastructure for foundation model training and inference, covering accelerated compute (NVIDIA GPU generations), high-bandwidth networking (NVLink, EFA), distributed storage, orchestration (Slurm, Kubernetes), and observability. The article introduces the 'three scaling laws' framing (pre-training, post-training, test-time compute) and maps how AWS building blocks interact with the OSS ML stack across the full model lifecycle.

A pithy quote from Boris Mann (via Simon Willison) arguing that counting AI agents — e.g. '11 AI agents' — is as meaningless as counting spreadsheets or browser tabs, skewering the tendency to treat agent count as a meaningful metric.

article.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat

harvestedTerms.summary: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announces 'Incognito Chat' for Meta AI — an end-to-end encrypted chat mode that stores no server-side logs, built on Meta's Private Processing technology. The article briefly compares temporary/incognito chat retention policies across Meta, Google (Gemini), OpenAI (ChatGPT), and Anthropic (Claude), and notes legal context around AI chat logs.

Anthropic launches Claude for Small Business, a packaged suite of agentic workflows and connectors built on Claude Cowork targeting SMBs. The article introduces a named product tier, a new course initiative, and reinforces several existing graph terms around connectors, workflows, and agentic deployment patterns.
